intent: Add collapsible Closed column to Kanban, showClosed toggle persisted in localStorage, visual quieting of rejected cards/rows/segments across all three views.
standards_checked: All state lives as a flat `applications` ref in `App.vue` — no Vuex/Pinia
Child components receive data via props and emit events back to `App.vue`

standards_compliance_summary.state_in_app_vue: PASS — showClosed, displayApplications, closedCount, pulseGhost, and pulseTimeout are all declared in App.vue script setup. No state added to child components.
standards_compliance_summary.props_and_emit_pattern: PASS — KanbanBoard, TableView, and TimelineView all receive showClosed/closedCount via props and emit 'toggle-show-closed' back to App.vue, which owns the toggleShowClosed handler. The intentional deviation (KanbanBoard receives full `applications` rather than `displayApplications`) is explicitly justified in a code comment explaining that vuedraggable requires all columns mounted in the DOM.
standards_compliance_summary.api_calls_through_api_js: PASS — No new API calls introduced in components. The `updateStatus` call in handleStatusChange continues to go through `./api`. The new `closedPartition.js` utility is pure client-side logic with no HTTP calls.
standards_compliance_summary.localstorage_key_naming: PASS — New key `jobtracker_show_closed` follows the existing `jobtracker_compact_header` pattern documented in CLAUDE.md (jobtracker_ prefix, snake_case suffix).

residual_risks: KanbanBoard receives the unfiltered `applications` prop while TableView and TimelineView receive `displayApplications`. This asymmetry is intentional (vuedraggable constraint) but is not documented in CLAUDE.md, so future developers may accidentally change it. The inline comment mitigates this.

The `closedPartition.js` utility is imported only by KanbanBoard. If TableView or TimelineView later need similar partitioning logic, the import pattern should be consistent — they should import from `../utils/` not inline the logic.
testing_gaps: The E2E test for showClosed persistence does not verify that a page reload (not just a view switch) preserves the preference — which is the core promise of localStorage persistence.

The unit test script added to package.json (`test:unit`) runs `closedPartition.test.js` directly with `node --test` but there is no equivalent for `timeline.test.js`. CLAUDE.md documents no automated test runner, so this is an inconsistency in test discoverability, not a standards violation.
